commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No automated repair job is needed; the patch has no blocking findings and should proceed through normal maintainer review, with duplicate PR cleanup after a canonical merge choice.; Cleared: Cleared: the diff adds no dependency or execution-surface changes, and the URL logging path remains sanitized while the music call site avoids local path disclosure. (sha=3126c8909c4a864ecae09e297a8e2716a4c75a9b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR passes operation and URL context to the music_generate reference fetch and Matrix guarded redirect timeout helper calls, adds a focused fetch-timeout test, and updates CHANGELOG.md.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows two production call sites omit `operation` and `url`, while `buildTimeoutAbortSignal` only emits those fields when supplied; the PR body also demonstrates the before/after helper behavior in a live Node process.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair job is needed; the patch has no blocking findings and should proceed through normal maintainer review, with duplicate PR cleanup after a canonical merge choice.
commands.reason: comment version already processed in ledger

commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a59a347cf8d2c668b39d2253d674d291545b0dcc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Slack mention target/source fields to prepared inbound context and prompt metadata, adds selective implicit-thread reply guidance, and updates Slack docs, changelog, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level with high confidence: current main computes explicit and implicit Slack mention facts, collapses them to WasMentioned/effectiveWasMentioned, and inbound prompt metadata emits only was_mentioned. The real Slack incident in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/79025 matches that path, though I did not run a live Slack workspace event.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer action is needed to fix the guidance leak and add redacted real behavior proof; automation should not merge while proof remains mock-only.
commands.reason: comment version already processed in ledger

commands.author: hclsys
commands.command: addressed the p2 finding: switched the fallback resolution path from bare `parsemodelref` (plain string split) to `buildmodelaliasindex` + `resolvemodelreffromstring` from `model-selection-shared.ts`, so aliases configured in `agents.defaults.models` expand correctly before the availability probe runs. added a 6th test case covering the alias expansion path. all 275 cron/isolated-agent tests pass. re-review welcome.
commands.freeform_prompt: Addressed the P2 finding: switched the fallback resolution path from bare `parseModelRef` (plain string split) to `buildModelAliasIndex` + `resolveModelRefFromString` from `model-selection-shared.ts`, so aliases configured in `agents.defaults.models` expand correctly before the availability probe runs. Added a 6th test case covering the alias expansion path. All 275 cron/isolated-agent tests pass. Re-review welcome.
commands.reason: author association CONTRIBUTOR and repository permission read are not allowed

commands.review_summary:
The branch adds orphan stream-json counters/logging, refreshes the Claude live-session idle timer on parsed orphan output, and adds focused orphan-output tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level failure path: current main drops parsed stream-json lines whenever no current turn is active, and the idle timer is only scheduled after turn finish. I did not establish a deterministic live Claude/Telegram reproduction during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor-supplied after-fix real behavior proof; automation cannot prove the contributor's Claude live-session and Telegram setup, and a changelog entry remains required.
